entries.name: Next.js
entries.role: React framework for full-stack web applications
entries.details: Next.js 16.1.6 is the core framework powering the application with App Router architecture. The project uses vanilla-extract plugin for CSS-in-JS styling and has custom rewrites for resume PDF generation. There are 129 files in the src/app directory including 40+ route handlers and pages.
facts: 129 files in src/app directory structure
40+ API routes and pages
Uses App Router architecture with layout.tsx and page.tsx conventions

entries.name: React
entries.role: UI component library
entries.details: React 19.1.1 is used throughout the application with 71 components in src/components directory. The project uses modern React patterns including providers, hooks, and server components. Components are organized into UI primitives, admin components, and domain-specific components.
facts: 71 component files in src/components directory
Uses React 19.1.1 with React DOM 19.1.1
Includes custom providers and hooks architecture

entries.name: Radix UI Themes
entries.role: Design system and component primitives
entries.details: Radix UI Themes 3.3.0 provides the design system foundation with Radix Icons 1.3.2. Used for building accessible UI components like Badge, Button, Card, and SearchInput in the components/ui directory. The theming system supports dark/light modes and consistent spacing.
facts: Radix UI Themes 3.3.0 with Radix Icons 1.3.2
Custom UI components in src/components/ui directory
Used for accessible form controls and layout primitives

entries.name: Clerk
entries.role: Authentication and user management
entries.details: Clerk 6.37.4 handles authentication with dedicated sign-in and sign-up pages. Integrated with Next.js App Router for protected routes and user session management. Provides auth headers and user context throughout the application.
facts: Clerk @clerk/nextjs version 6.37.4
Dedicated /sign-in and /sign-up routes
Auth API route at /api/auth/[...all]

entries.name: Vanilla Extract
entries.role: Zero-runtime CSS-in-JS styling
entries.details: Vanilla Extract 1.18.0 provides type-safe CSS-in-JS with zero runtime. Integrated via Next.js plugin in next.config.ts. Used for component styling with CSS modules approach, particularly in jobs-list.css.ts and source-filter.css.ts.
facts: @vanilla-extract/css version 1.18.0
Integrated via Next.js plugin in next.config.ts
Used for type-safe CSS modules

entries.name: React Flow
entries.role: Interactive node-based diagrams and flowcharts
entries.details: React Flow (via @xyflow/react) 12.10.1 powers interactive node-based visualizations for study and preparation flows. Used in prep components for visualizing topic relationships and progress tracking with custom node components.
facts: @xyflow/react version 12.10.1
Used for study and preparation flow visualizations
Custom node components in src/components/prep/nodes

entries.name: Apollo Server
entries.role: GraphQL server implementation
entries.details: Provides GraphQL API with modular schema design across 34 GraphQL files organized by domain (applications, companies, contacts, jobs, etc.). Context includes userId, userEmail, db instance, and loaders. The GraphQL endpoint is at /api/graphql route.
facts: 34 GraphQL schema files organized across 11 domains
GraphQL context includes userId, userEmail, db instance, and loaders
41 API route files in src/app/api/**/* structure

source_locations.note: Base Query and Mutation type definitions

entries.name: Next.js API Routes
entries.role: RESTful API endpoints
entries.details: Provides 41 API route files organized by functionality including admin, auth, companies, emails, webhooks, and resume PDF generation. Each route follows the App Router pattern with route.ts files. Vercel configuration sets 60-second max duration for all API functions.
facts: 41 API route files across 10 functional areas
Vercel timeout configured to 60 seconds max duration
Includes admin, auth, companies, emails, webhooks, and resume PDF endpoints

entries.name: GraphQL Schema
entries.role: Type definitions and operations
entries.details: Modular schema design with base types extended across 11 domains. Includes custom scalars (JSON, DateTime, URL, EmailAddress, Upload) and enums for source types and extraction methods. Schema organized in /schema directory with separate files for queries, mutations, and type definitions.
facts: 11 domain-specific schema modules extending base types
5 custom scalars: JSON, DateTime, URL, EmailAddress, Upload
2 enums: SourceType and ExtractMethod with 5 values each

source_locations.note: Base schema with scalars and enums

entries.name: Cloudflare D1
entries.role: Serverless SQLite database for job listings and user data
entries.details: Used as the primary database with D1 binding 'DB' connecting to 'nomadically-db' (ID: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b). Accessed via a dedicated gateway worker at workers/d1-gateway.ts that provides RESTful API endpoints for jobs, user settings, and companies. The database has 31 migration files and contains tables for jobs, companies, user_settings, job_skill_tags, skill_aliases, company_facts, and ashby_boards.
facts: Database ID: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b
31 SQL migration files in migrations/ directory
7 main tables defined in src/db/schema.ts

entries.name: Drizzle ORM
entries.role: Type-safe SQL ORM for schema definition and query building
entries.details: Used to define the database schema in src/db/schema.ts with 7 main tables: companies, jobs, ashby_boards, user_settings, job_skill_tags, skill_aliases, and company_facts. Provides type inference with $inferSelect and $inferInsert. The schema includes indexes, foreign keys, and JSON fields. Drizzle is imported from 'drizzle-orm/sqlite-core' and used with SQLite dialect for D1 compatibility.
facts: 7 tables defined in schema.ts
Uses sqliteTable from drizzle-orm/sqlite-core
Includes 6 indexes on jobs table alone

entries.name: D1 Gateway Worker
entries.role: REST API gateway for secure D1 database access from Vercel
entries.details: Custom Cloudflare Worker at workers/d1-gateway.ts that exposes specific query patterns as HTTP endpoints. Uses Bearer token authentication with API_KEY env var. Provides endpoints for /jobs, /jobs/:id, /jobs/batch, /user-settings, and /companies. Implements CORS, caching headers, and batched queries for performance. Worker is configured in wrangler.d1-gateway.toml with observability enabled.
facts: 5 main API endpoints exposed
Uses Bearer token authentication
Configured with compatibility_date: 2026-02-01

entries.name: DeepSeek via @ai-sdk/deepseek
entries.role: Primary LLM for job classification, SQL generation, and email generation
entries.details: Used throughout the project via @ai-sdk/deepseek package. Configured for remote EU job classification in the process-jobs worker (runs hourly via cron), SQL generation agents, and email generation. The system uses multiple DeepSeek models including deepseek-chat, deepseek-reasoner, and deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-32b via Cloudflare Workers AI. Base URL configured as https://api.deepseek.com with beta features available at https://api.deepseek.com/beta.
facts: 6 agents use DeepSeek for SQL generation and admin tasks
Process-jobs worker runs hourly via cron (0 * * * *) for job classification
Uses DEEPSEEK_API_KEY environment variable for authentication

source_locations.note: DeepSeek import for SQL generation agent
Hourly cron schedule for job classification

entries.name: Anthropic Claude via @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
entries.role: AI agent SDK for autonomous job seeking, architect sub-agents, and study topics
entries.details: Integrated through @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk package with project settings in .claude/ directory and CLAUDE.md. Used for job seeking agents, architect sub-agents, and study topic generation with models like claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Includes MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for PostgreSQL, filesystem, and GitHub integration.
facts: 16 files in src/anthropic/ directory for Claude integration
Supports models: claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, claude-haiku-3-5-20241022
Uses .claude/ directory for project settings and skills

source_locations.note: Anthropic import for study topic generation

entries.name: Vercel AI SDK
entries.role: Unified interface for AI models across the application
entries.details: Provides a unified interface for AI models across the application via the 'ai' package. Used alongside @ai-sdk/deepseek and @ai-sdk/anthropic packages. Enables consistent model calling patterns for SQL generation, study topics, and resume analysis across different LLM providers.
facts: Combined with @ai-sdk/deepseek and @ai-sdk/anthropic packages
Used in 6 agent files for consistent AI model calling
Provides unified streaming and completion interfaces

source_locations.note: AI SDK usage with Claude model
AI SDK usage with DeepSeek model

entries.name: OpenRouter via @ai-sdk/openai
entries.role: Gateway for testing multiple models and fallback provider
entries.details: Acts as a fallback and comparison gateway when testing multiple models against the same prompt. Configured with baseURL: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1. Provides access to multiple DeepSeek models including deepseek-chat, deepseek-r1, deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-32b, and deepseek-coder through a unified interface.
facts: Uses OpenAI-compatible API interface via @ai-sdk/openai
Provides access to 5 different DeepSeek models through OpenRouter
Configured in src/openrouter/provider.ts with OpenRouter base URL

entries.name: Google ADK
entries.role: Google AI development kit for Gemini model integration
entries.details: Google AI development kit for Gemini model integration. Includes both @google/adk and @google/adk-devtools packages. Provides structured access to Google's AI models alongside other LLM providers in the application.
facts: Includes both @google/adk and @google/adk-devtools packages
Part of multi-provider LLM strategy alongside DeepSeek and Claude
Version 0.3.0 indicates early adoption/beta usage

entries.name: Langfuse
entries.role: Central LLM observability with tracing, prompt versioning, and scoring
entries.details: Langfuse is configured as the primary observability layer with LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, and LANGFUSE_BASE_URL environment variables pointing to https://cloud.langfuse.com. It's used for runtime prompt management (fetching prompts like 'job-classifier'), tracing all LLM calls, and scoring accuracy via evaluation scripts. Prompts are referenced using '@@@langfusePrompt:name=PromptName|label=production@@@' format and fetched dynamically from Langfuse cloud.
facts: Configured in src/config/env.ts with 3 required environment variables
Used in 3 evaluation scripts: eval-remote-eu-langfuse.ts, classify-remote-eu-jobs.ts, and build-golden-datasets.py
Prompt references use format '@@@langfusePrompt:name=PromptName|label=production@@@'

entries.name: LangSmith
entries.role: Alternative LLM evaluation and tracing platform
entries.details: LangSmith is installed as a dependency but appears to be used primarily as a fallback or alternative evaluation platform. The project has a script 'eval:langfuse' that runs evaluations against both Langfuse and LangSmith. It's integrated into remote EU evaluation workflows with dedicated scorer functions for tracking LLM performance on classification tasks.
facts: Installed as dependency version 0.5.2 in package.json
Has dedicated eval script 'eval:langfuse' for evaluations
Used in workers/eu-classifier and workers/process-jobs Python modules

entries.name: Cloudflare Workers
entries.role: Serverless compute platform for background jobs and cron tasks
entries.details: Multiple workers handle different tasks: ashby-crawler (Rust/WASM) runs daily at 02:00 UTC to crawl job boards, eu-classifier (Python) runs every 6 hours for role classification, and cleanup-jobs processes queue messages. Workers use D1 database bindings to shared 'nomadically-work-db' (ID: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b) and queue bindings for job processing.
facts: 5 workers have cron triggers (free plan limit reached)
All workers share same D1 database with remote bindings
3 Rust/WASM workers, 5 Python workers, 2 TypeScript workers

entries.name: Rust/WASM (worker-build)
entries.role: High-performance crawlers and agents compiled to WebAssembly
entries.details: Used in ashby-crawler, sdd-agent, and other performance-critical workers. Built with worker-build toolchain and deployed as WASM modules. ashby-crawler specifically crawls Common Crawl CDX API for Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, and Workable job boards with 5 pages per provider per run.
facts: 3 Rust/WASM workers: ashby-crawler, sdd-agent, and one more
Uses worker-build command for compilation
13 source files in ashby-crawler/src directory

source_locations.note: Build command: cargo install -q worker-build && worker-build --release

entries.name: Python Workers
entries.role: AI/ML processing and job classification with LangChain
entries.details: Python workers like eu-classifier, cleanup-jobs, job-reporter-llm use python_workers compatibility flag. eu-classifier uses Cloudflare AI bindings and LangChain for role classification with environment variables for Langfuse tracing (LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true).
facts: 5 Python workers identified
All use compatibility_flags: ["python_workers"]
eu-classifier has AI binding for Cloudflare AI models

entries.name: Cloudflare Queues
entries.role: Message queue system for async job processing
entries.details: Used for decoupled processing: job-report-queue (max_batch_size: 10), cleanup-jobs-queue (max_batch_size: 1), with dead letter queues for failed messages. Queue bindings allow producers and consumers to communicate between workers.
facts: 3 queues configured: job-report-queue, cleanup-jobs-queue, plus others
All queues have DLQ (dead letter queue) configurations
Max retries typically set to 3

entries.name: Cloudflare D1
entries.role: SQLite-based database for all worker data
entries.details: Shared database 'nomadically-work-db' (ID: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b) used by all workers with remote bindings for local development. Contains job listings, classifications, and processing state.
facts: Single database ID used across all workers: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b
All workers use 'DB' binding name
Remote binding enabled for local dev

entries.name: Trigger.dev
entries.role: Background job orchestration for job data enhancement and processing
entries.details: Used for scheduled and on-demand background jobs to enhance job postings from ATS sources like Greenhouse and Ashby. Configured in trigger.config.ts with project ID 'proj_gmqcwyqsqcnkjnlqcmxf', runtime 'node', and tasks located in ./src/trigger directory. Tasks include job enhancement with retry logic, scheduled cron jobs, and resume processing.
facts: Found 5 task files in src/trigger/ directory
Configured with cron schedule '0 */6 * * *' for scheduled job enhancement
Tasks have concurrency limit of 5 and max duration of 120-300 seconds
Uses batchTriggerAndWait for fan-out pattern to process up to 200 jobs

entries.name: Langfuse
entries.role: Observability and scoring platform for live evaluations
entries.details: Used for live evaluation tracking with scorers attached to agents. Automatically tracks evaluations in Langfuse for observability. Integrated with Mastra for scorer functionality. Documentation referenced in src/evals/index.eval.ts for Langfuse Scores.
facts: Langfuse used for live evaluation scoring
Scorers attached to agents in src/agents/
Langfuse Scores documentation referenced in eval files

source_locations.note: Mention of Langfuse for automatic tracking
